svg.text: Pogo Pin Mount (Dock Ceiling Bracket) — Engineering Drawing
Material: PETG bracket + FR4 PCB | Envelope: 60W x 30D x 10H mm | 3-6 pogo pins (P75/P100) | Scale noted per view
1. Material: PETG bracket, FR4 PCB (1.6mm nominal, shown as 2mm with solder mask). Conformal coating (silicone or acrylic) on all PCB traces;
contact pads left bare metal for electrical contact.
2. Pins: 3x required (GND, Motor A 12V, Motor B 12V), up to 3x optional (cartridge ID, temp sensor, spare). P75 or P100 series, 2-3mm dia, 1-2mm stroke.
3. Pin spacing: 10mm center-to-center. Bracket centered at X=106 within 212mm enclosure interior (centered on 148mm cartridge width).
4. Target pads on cartridge top face: 8mm x 5mm each, nickel-plated brass, 10mm c-t-c. Oversized pads tolerate 2-3mm misalignment.
5. Wipe action: pin tip drags across elongated pad during slide-in insertion, self-cleaning oxide films and debris.
6. Drainage channel molded into dock ceiling slopes away from pin pockets. Electrical contacts on dock CEILING (top), water fittings on REAR — gravity separates moisture.
7. Current: ~0.85A typical per motor, ~3A stall transient. Even P75 probes (1-3A rated) are adequate; Mill-Max 0852 (9A) for extra margin.
8. Mounting: M3 screws into heat-set inserts in dock ceiling. Bracket at Z=80-84mm (top of cartridge slot).
9. Spring clearance: minimum 4mm below bracket bottom face to plunger tip at full extension (uncompressed). Cartridge top face contacts plunger at ~1-2mm compression.
10. Optional pins shown with dashed outlines. 6th pin position omitted from drawing — add if needed.
Tolerances: +/- 0.2mm (3D printed bracket), +/- 0.1mm (PCB features) | All dimensions in mm | Drawing not to production scale — verify against physical parts
